commonIssues:
15% of applications are rejected due to incorrect, missing, or oversized files.

How to fix:

  • Verify the checklist on the Dharani portal and ensure all files are in the correct format before uploading. Dharani Portal

Mistakes in applicant names, land area, or market values in the application compared to the Sale Deed, often caused by reliance on agents.

How to fix:

  • Cross-check the application draft against the original Sale Deed and Aadhaar before final submission.

Difficulty in uploading documents due to size constraints.

How to fix:

  • Compress documents below 500 KB using standard PDF compression tools.

Applications get stuck at the "Pending at L1" stage, typically due to missing information, FTL restrictions, or prohibited land status.

How to fix:

  • Visit the Tahsildar/MRO office to resolve FTL or prohibitory land restrictions causing the L1 hold.

Incorrect land valuation leading to exorbitant penalties (e.g., ₹2,00,000 instead of ₹20,000).

How to fix:

  • Verify the exact market value of the land on the Dharani portal prior to payment to avoid deficit penalties.

Despite digital transition, many face prolonged delays in NALA (Non-Agricultural Land Assessment) for land conversion.

How to fix:

  • Apply for NALA conversion via Dharani and track the status. Escalate to the RDO if delayed.

Unclear titles due to agricultural land-ceiling limits, protected tenancy rights, or Inam/Jagir labels.

How to fix:

  • Apply for the removal of obsolete Inam/Jagir tags by submitting proof of ownership to the Revenue Divisional Officer (RDO).

Many applicants used agents who became unreachable after submission.

How to fix:

  • Use the citizen services on the Dharani portal directly or visit official MeeSeva centers. MeeSeva
